description_long: This object is a striking cluster of elongated, prismatic rock crystals radiating outward from a dense central base. The crystals are sharply faceted, with well-defined hexagonal cross-sections and pointed terminations, giving the formation a dramatic, spiky appearance. The surface texture is hard and slightly glossy, with subtle striations running along the length of each crystal. The coloration is predominantly a dark, metallic gray, suggesting a mineral such as quartz or hematite. The overall structure is irregular, with some crystals jutting out at steep angles, creating a visually complex and rugged silhouette. This type of rock formation could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A dramatic cluster of elongated, dark gray prismatic crystals with sharp, faceted surfaces and a rugged, spiky silhouette. The rock has a hard, slightly glossy finish and an irregular, radiating structure.
description_view_0: From this angle, the tallest crystals are prominent, rising steeply from the dense base and showing their pointed terminations.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the horizontal spread of the crystal cluster, revealing more of the radiating structure and the varying lengths of the crystals.
description_view_2: Here, the dense base and the parallel alignment of many crystals are visible, emphasizing the vertical stacking and close packing.
description_view_3: This view shows the cluster from above, displaying the radial arrangement and the sharp, angular points of the crystals.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped plaza as a decorative or barrier element
